(302)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, A PROTECTIVE ORDER; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS HEARING SET FOR FEB-26-2014 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOS OT MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSTIION FILED BY DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET THREE AND DOCUMENT DEMANDS, SET THREE AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST ARMADA IN THE AMOUNT OF $4900.00 IS GRANTED. WITHIN 15 DAYS, DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES, INC. SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO TRAVELERS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 35, 36, 37, 38, AND 39 AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69, 70, AND 71. THESE DISCOVERY REQUESTS ARE RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE PENDING ACTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES, INC.'S RESPONSES THAT CONSIST SOLELY OF OBJECTIONS ARE A MISUSE OF DISCOVERY. (DEYO V. KILBOURNE (1978) 84 CAL. APP. 3D 771, 783.) MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $ 4,900.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA AND AGAINST DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES, INC. AND THEIR COUNSEL, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-26-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

ATTY. SCOTT L. WOODALL, TEL. NO. (925) 600-0111 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's Motion To Compel Defendant'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Request For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00 is Granted. Within 15 days, Defendant Armada Protective Services, Inc. shall provide further verified responses, without objection, to Travelers' Request for Production Nos. 35, 36, 37, 38, and 39 and Special Interrogatory nos. 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69, 70, and 71. These discovery requests are relevant to the subject matter of the pending action. The Court finds that Defendant Armada Protective Services, Inc.'s responses that consist solely of objections are a misuse of discovery. (Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal. App. 3d 771, 783.) Monetary sanctions of $ 4,900.00 are awarded in favor of Travelers Casualty Insurance Company of America and against Defendant Armada Protective Services, Inc. and their counsel, jointly and severally, to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
